examples.en: She has worked as a software developer for five years.
examples.zh: 她當軟體開發者已經五年了。

examples.en: Our team is developing a new mobile game this year.
examples.zh: 我們團隊今年正在開發一款新手遊。

examples.en: The game is played from a first-person perspective.
examples.zh: 這款遊戲是第一人稱視角。

examples.en: I prefer FPS games with a first-person perspective.
examples.zh: 我比較喜歡用第一人稱視角的射擊遊戲。

examples.en: I bought a new video game console last weekend.
examples.zh: 我上週末買了一台新的遊戲主機。

examples.en: The Switch is a popular video game console for families.
examples.zh: Switch 是很受家庭歡迎的遊戲主機。

examples.en: The sensor can detect body movement very accurately.
examples.zh: 感應器可以非常精準地偵測身體動作。

examples.en: These cameras are designed to detect body movement at night.
examples.zh: 這些攝影機是設計來在夜間偵測身體動作的。

examples.en: The Switch supports motion control through its Joy-Con.
examples.zh: Switch 透過 Joy-Con 支援體感控制。

examples.en: Motion control makes some sports games much more fun.
examples.zh: 體感控制讓一些運動類遊戲變得更好玩。

examples.en: He built an AI learning assistant for English learners.
examples.zh: 他打造了一個給英文學習者用的 AI 學習助理。

examples.en: A good learning assistant should adjust to your level.
examples.zh: 好的學習助理應該要能配合你的程度。

examples.en: You can choose the level before each practice session.
examples.zh: 每次練習前你可以選擇程度。

examples.en: I usually choose the level that's just a bit harder than mine.
examples.zh: 我通常會選擇比自己程度再難一點的等級。

examples.en: I learn English from podcasts and YouTube materials every day.
examples.zh: 我每天用 podcast 跟 YouTube 教學素材學英文。

examples.en: She mixes podcasts with YouTube materials to keep things fresh.
examples.zh: 她把 podcast 和 YouTube 教學素材交替使用，避免無聊。

examples.en: Daily exercise is beneficial to both body and mind.
examples.zh: 每天運動對身心都有益。

examples.en: Reading aloud is beneficial for pronunciation practice.
examples.zh: 朗讀對發音練習很有幫助。

examples.en: Some inventions can be both useful and destructive.
examples.zh: 有些發明既實用又具破壞性。

examples.en: The storm caused destructive damage to the coastal area.
examples.zh: 這場風暴對沿海地區造成了破壞性的損害。

examples.en: Every technology has its advantages and disadvantages.
examples.zh: 每項科技都有它的優點與缺點。

examples.en: Working from home has more advantages than disadvantages for me.
examples.zh: 在家工作對我來說優點比缺點多。

examples.en: Wash your hands often to avoid spreading bacteria.
examples.zh: 常洗手以避免散播細菌。

examples.en: Germs spread easily in crowded places.
examples.zh: 病菌在擁擠的地方很容易傳播。

examples.en: I need an hour to analyze a report before the meeting.
examples.zh: 會議前我需要一小時來分析報告。

examples.en: AI tools can help us analyze a report much faster.
examples.zh: AI 工具可以幫我們更快地分析報告。

lesson-006::lesson-006-review::phrase::8.term: compare new data with old data

examples.en: We always compare new data with old data to spot trends.
examples.zh: 我們總是把新舊資料比較，以找出趨勢。

examples.en: The system will compare new data with old data automatically.
examples.zh: 這個系統會自動比較新舊資料。

examples.en: Let's focus on the latest data from this month.
examples.zh: 我們來聚焦本月最新的資料。

examples.en: Sometimes you need to focus on the latest data, not historical numbers.
examples.zh: 有時候你要聚焦最新資料，而不是歷史數據。

examples.en: The party started to get out of control after midnight.
examples.zh: 派對在午夜後開始失控。

examples.en: We have to act before the situation gets out of control.
examples.zh: 我們得在情況失控前行動。

examples.en: There was a huge traffic jam on the highway this morning.
examples.zh: 今天早上高速公路大塞車。

examples.en: I always leave home early to avoid the traffic jam.
examples.zh: 我總是提早出門避開塞車。

examples.en: Overpopulation puts pressure on natural resources.
examples.zh: 人口過剩會對自然資源造成壓力。

examples.en: Some rural towns face serious depopulation.
examples.zh: 一些鄉下小鎮面臨嚴重的人口減少問題。

examples.en: Population ageing is one of Taiwan's biggest challenges.
examples.zh: 人口老化是台灣最大的挑戰之一。

examples.en: Many countries are designing policies to deal with population ageing.
examples.zh: 許多國家在設計政策因應人口老化。

examples.en: Doctors can implant a small chip to monitor heart activity.
examples.zh: 醫生可以植入一個小晶片來監測心臟活動。

examples.en: She received a cochlear implant last year.
examples.zh: 她去年植入了人工電子耳。

examples.en: The snake-like robot can squeeze through narrow pipes.
examples.zh: 這個蛇形機器人可以鑽過狹窄的管道。

examples.en: Engineers use a snake-like robot to inspect old buildings.
examples.zh: 工程師用蛇形機器人來檢查老舊建築。

examples.en: The slender robotic arm can reach into tight spaces.
examples.zh: 細長的機器手臂可以伸進狹小空間。

examples.en: Surgeons use a slender robotic arm for delicate operations.
examples.zh: 外科醫師用細長的機器手臂進行精細手術。

examples.en: These cameras can record video underwater for hours.
examples.zh: 這些攝影機可以在水下錄影好幾個小時。

examples.en: The underwater world is full of strange creatures.
examples.zh: 水下的世界充滿奇怪的生物。

examples.en: Astronauts spend months living in outer space.
examples.zh: 太空人在外太空生活好幾個月。

examples.en: Sending equipment to outer space is extremely expensive.
examples.zh: 把設備送到外太空非常昂貴。

examples.en: Their lab is doing cutting edge research on AI safety.
examples.zh: 他們的實驗室在做尖端的 AI 安全研究。

examples.en: This phone uses cutting edge camera technology.
examples.zh: 這款手機使用尖端的相機技術。

examples.en: The car body is made from carbon-fibre composite.
examples.zh: 車身是由碳纖維複合材料製成的。

examples.en: Composite materials are lighter and stronger than steel.
examples.zh: 複合材料比鋼鐵更輕也更堅固。

examples.en: A lighter chassis improves fuel efficiency.
examples.zh: 更輕的底盤可以提升油耗效率。

examples.en: The chassis of this car is built from carbon fibre.
examples.zh: 這款車的底盤是用碳纖維打造的。

examples.en: Many car makers focus on low emissions to meet new rules.
examples.zh: 許多車廠專注低排放以符合新法規。

examples.en: Hybrid engines produce low emissions and save fuel.
examples.zh: 油電混合引擎排放低又省油。

examples.en: She is an icon of the music industry.
examples.zh: 她是音樂界的標誌性人物。

examples.en: The Statue of Liberty is an icon of New York.
examples.zh: 自由女神像是紐約的標誌。

examples.en: Their headquarters are located in central London.
examples.zh: 他們的總部位於倫敦市中心。

examples.en: The company is moving its headquarters next month.
examples.zh: 這家公司下個月要搬遷總部。

examples.en: Rolls-Royce is famous for its technical innovation.
examples.zh: 勞斯萊斯以技術創新聞名。

examples.en: Technical innovation often comes from solving real problems.
examples.zh: 技術創新通常來自解決真實問題。

examples.en: He started his career as an apprentice at sixteen.
examples.zh: 他十六歲就開始當學徒。

examples.en: An apprenticeship usually lasts several years.
examples.zh: 學徒期通常持續好幾年。

examples.en: Let's take it for a spin before we decide.
examples.zh: 我們先試駕一下再決定。

examples.en: He took the new bike for a spin around the park.
examples.zh: 他騎新的自行車在公園繞了一圈。

examples.en: He was promoted to the bank robbery department.
examples.zh: 他被升職到銀行搶案部門。

examples.en: The bank robbery department handles high-profile cases.
examples.zh: 銀行搶案部門負責高關注度的案件。

examples.en: Your partner watches your back; your boss watches your performance.
examples.zh: 你的搭檔保護你的背後，你的老闆看你的表現。

examples.en: He just got promoted, so now he's also my boss, not just my partner.
examples.zh: 他剛升職，所以他現在不只是我搭檔，也是我的老闆。

examples.en: She got a promotion last week.
examples.zh: 她上週升職了。

examples.en: A promotion usually means more responsibility.
examples.zh: 升職通常代表更多的責任。

examples.en: Detectives do a lot of field work outside the office.
examples.zh: 偵探做很多在辦公室外的實地工作。

examples.en: I prefer field work over staying at my desk all day.
examples.zh: 我比較喜歡實地工作，不想整天坐辦公桌。

examples.en: Please show some form of identification at the front desk.
examples.zh: 請在櫃檯出示身分證件。

examples.en: Witness identification was the key to the case.
examples.zh: 證人身分確認是這案件的關鍵。

examples.en: Let me check my schedule and get back to you.
examples.zh: 我查一下行程再回覆你。

examples.en: We're running behind schedule because of the rain.
examples.zh: 因為下雨我們進度落後了。

examples.en: I listen to a podcast on my way to work every morning.
examples.zh: 我每天通勤路上聽 podcast。

examples.en: There are great English-learning podcasts for free.
examples.zh: 有很多很棒的英文學習 podcast 是免費的。

examples.en: Let's continue the story next class.
examples.zh: 我們下堂課繼續這個故事。

examples.en: She asked the children to continue the story in their own words.
examples.zh: 她要小朋友用自己的話繼續這個故事。

examples.en: Let's get the ball rolling on the new project.
examples.zh: 我們開始進行這個新專案吧。

examples.en: He sent the first email to get the ball rolling.
examples.zh: 他寄了第一封信讓事情開始動起來。

examples.en: It took me a few months to learn the ropes at this company.
examples.zh: 我花了幾個月才在這間公司熟悉工作流程。

examples.en: Don't worry — your manager will help you learn the ropes.
examples.zh: 別擔心，你的主管會幫你熟悉做法。

examples.en: I'm still pretty green at this job, so I ask a lot of questions.
examples.zh: 我在這份工作還很生疏，所以會問很多問題。

examples.en: Even an experienced engineer feels green at a new job at first.
examples.zh: 就算是有經驗的工程師，剛到新工作也會覺得生疏。

examples.en: Marketing strategy is outside of my expertise.
examples.zh: 行銷策略不是我的專業。

examples.en: I'd rather not answer — that's outside of my expertise.
examples.zh: 我不太想回答這個，那不是我的專業領域。

examples.en: He started as an apprentice in his uncle's bakery.
examples.zh: 他從他叔叔的麵包店學徒做起。

examples.en: Many crafts still rely on the apprentice system.
examples.zh: 許多手工業仍然依賴學徒制度。

examples.en: She is a master of classical guitar.
examples.zh: 她是古典吉他的大師。

examples.en: It takes years to become a master at any skill.
examples.zh: 要成為任何技能的大師都要花上很多年。

examples.en: Reading every night is a good habit.
examples.zh: 每晚閱讀是個好習慣。

examples.en: Old habits are hard to break.
examples.zh: 舊習慣很難改。

examples.en: Finding the right motivation is half the battle.
examples.zh: 找到正確的動機就成功了一半。

examples.en: Money is not always the best motivation.
examples.zh: 金錢未必是最好的動機。

examples.en: I feel more motivated when I see real progress.
examples.zh: 看到真的進步時我會更有動力。

examples.en: She's a highly motivated student.
examples.zh: 她是個非常有動力的學生。

examples.en: His English gradually improved over the year.
examples.zh: 他的英文在這一年裡逐漸進步。

examples.en: Try to gradually increase the difficulty.
examples.zh: 試著逐漸提高難度。

examples.en: That comedian is really funny.
examples.zh: 那位喜劇演員真的很好笑。

examples.en: It's not funny when the joke goes too far.
examples.zh: 玩笑開過頭就不好笑了。

examples.en: I missed the punchline because I was looking at my phone.
examples.zh: 我看手機所以錯過了笑點。

examples.en: A good punchline catches you off guard.
examples.zh: 好的笑點會讓你猝不及防。

examples.en: I've been into science fiction since high school.
examples.zh: 我從高中起就很喜歡科幻。

examples.en: Most sci-fi movies imagine a future with advanced technology.
examples.zh: 大多數科幻電影都在想像有先進科技的未來。

examples.en: What's your favourite movie genre?
examples.zh: 你最喜歡的電影類型是什麼？

examples.en: Science fiction is my favourite genre.
examples.zh: 科幻是我最愛的類型。

examples.en: I listen to audiobooks while I cook.
examples.zh: 我邊煮飯邊聽有聲書。

examples.en: Audiobooks are great for improving listening skills.
examples.zh: 有聲書很適合用來提升聽力。

examples.en: I usually watch movies with English subtitles.
examples.zh: 我看電影通常會開英文字幕。

examples.en: Try to watch without subtitles for a real challenge.
examples.zh: 試試看不開字幕，挑戰一下自己。

examples.en: Building a strong network takes years.
examples.zh: 建立強大的人脈需要好幾年。

examples.en: She has a wide network in the tech industry.
examples.zh: 她在科技業有很廣的人脈。

examples.en: I had lunch with a colleague today.
examples.zh: 我今天跟一位同事吃午餐。

examples.en: She's a great colleague — always willing to help.
examples.zh: 她是個很棒的同事，總是樂於幫忙。

examples.en: We were classmates back in high school.
examples.zh: 我們高中時是同學。

examples.en: I'm still in touch with a few classmates from college.
examples.zh: 我還跟幾位大學同學保持聯絡。

examples.en: There's a tech meetup downtown every Thursday.
examples.zh: 市中心每週四都有科技聚會。

examples.en: I joined a language meetup to practise my English.
examples.zh: 我參加了一個語言聚會練英文。

examples.en: I found a conversation partner online to practise English.
examples.zh: 我在網路上找到一位對話夥伴練英文。

examples.en: A good conversation partner is patient and curious.
examples.zh: 好的對話夥伴有耐心又有好奇心。

examples.en: I read a short story before going to bed.
examples.zh: 我睡前讀了一篇短篇故事。

examples.en: Short stories are good for English learners.
examples.zh: 短篇故事很適合英文學習者。

examples.en: She's writing her first novel.
examples.zh: 她正在寫她的第一本小說。

examples.en: That novel was made into a movie last year.
examples.zh: 那本小說去年被改編成電影。

examples.en: A graded reader is a book written for a specific level.
examples.zh: 分級讀本是針對特定程度寫的書。

examples.en: Start with a graded reader before tackling original novels.
examples.zh: 在挑戰原文小說前，先從分級讀本開始。

examples.en: I didn't recognize him at first.
examples.zh: 我一開始沒認出他來。

examples.en: It took me a while to recognize the pattern.
examples.zh: 我花了一陣子才看出規律。

examples.en: I made a mental note to email him later.
examples.zh: 我心中記下要晚點寄信給他。

examples.en: She made a mental note of every name in the room.
examples.zh: 她把房間裡每個人的名字都記在心裡。

examples.en: Make sure you have the correct version of the file.
examples.zh: 確認你拿到的是檔案的正確版本。

examples.en: Here's the correct version of the report.
examples.zh: 這是報告的正確版本。

examples.en: His face looked familiar but I couldn't remember his name.
examples.zh: 他的臉看起來很熟，但我想不起名字。

examples.en: Are you familiar with this software?
examples.zh: 你熟悉這個軟體嗎？

examples.en: I had a strange dream last night.
examples.zh: 我昨晚做了個奇怪的夢。

examples.en: Everything feels strange in a new city.
examples.zh: 在新城市裡所有事情都讓人陌生。

examples.en: Pause for a moment to think before answering.
examples.zh: 回答前先停頓一下思考。

examples.en: She paused after every important sentence.
examples.zh: 她在每個重要句子後都會停頓。

examples.en: Text-to-speech is useful for learners who want to hear pronunciation.
examples.zh: 文字轉語音對想聽發音的學習者很有用。

examples.en: Modern TTS voices sound almost human.
examples.zh: 現代的 TTS 聲音幾乎像真人。

examples.en: Speech-to-text helps me write notes faster.
examples.zh: 語音轉文字幫我更快寫筆記。

examples.en: Speech-to-text accuracy has improved a lot in recent years.
examples.zh: 語音轉文字的準確度近幾年大幅提升。

examples.en: She's been seeing a psychiatrist for her anxiety.
examples.zh: 她因為焦慮在看精神科醫師。

examples.en: A psychiatrist can prescribe medication; a therapist usually can't.
examples.zh: 精神科醫師可以開藥，治療師通常不行。

examples.en: I review English with flashcards every morning.
examples.zh: 我每天早上用單字卡複習英文。

examples.en: Flashcards are simple but very effective.
examples.zh: 單字卡很簡單但非常有效。

examples.en: Look up the definition if you're not sure.
examples.zh: 不確定的話就查一下定義。

examples.en: The dictionary gives a clear definition.
examples.zh: 字典提供了清楚的定義。

examples.en: The translation is a little different from the original.
examples.zh: 翻譯跟原文有點不一樣。

examples.en: I prefer reading without relying on translation.
examples.zh: 我喜歡讀的時候不要依賴翻譯。

examples.en: Knowing the part of speech helps you use a word correctly.
examples.zh: 知道詞性可以幫助你正確使用單字。

examples.en: Is 'run' a noun or a verb? It depends on the part of speech.
examples.zh: 'run' 是名詞還是動詞？要看詞性。

examples.en: What level are you at in English?
examples.zh: 你的英文程度在哪個級別？

examples.en: Pick a book that matches your level.
examples.zh: 選擇符合你程度的書。

examples.en: If you organise your time well, studying feels easier.
examples.zh: 如果你安排好時間，讀書會輕鬆許多。

examples.en: It's hard to organise your time when there are too many tasks.
examples.zh: 任務太多時就很難安排時間。

examples.en: Background music sometimes hurts my productivity.
examples.zh: 背景音樂有時會降低我的生產力。

examples.en: Short breaks can boost productivity.
examples.zh: 短暫休息可以提升生產力。

examples.en: I keep a short to-do list every morning.
examples.zh: 我每天早上都會列一份短的待辦清單。

examples.en: Crossing items off a to-do list feels great.
examples.zh: 在待辦清單上劃掉項目感覺真好。

examples.en: Break down a task into small steps to make it manageable.
examples.zh: 把任務拆解成小步驟比較容易管理。

examples.en: She broke down a task and tackled one piece at a time.
examples.zh: 她把任務拆解後，一次處理一個部分。

examples.en: Pomodoro is great for short bursts of intensive work.
examples.zh: 番茄鐘很適合短時間的高強度專注工作。

examples.en: Two hours of intensive work beats six hours of distracted effort.
examples.zh: 兩小時的高強度工作勝過六小時分心做事。
